summary: Open fighter decompilation PR touching four FT files. PR body claims exact-match decompilation of 4 newly matched functions, verified in isolation with ninja changes_all and clean regression report; bot report lists 4 new matches and 8 additional unmatched-item improvements.

key_files.role: Largest change; refactors ftColl_80077970 into inline helpers, relocates .sdata2 constant definitions after the matched function with extern declarations earlier, and improves ftColl_80077C60.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftcoll.c .sdata2 extern declarations and later definitions
diff_excerpt: ftColl_80077970 introduces inlineItemA0 and inlineItemA1
text_corpus issue_comment: ftColl_8007925C and ftColl_80077970 reach 100%; ftColl_80077C60 improves to 98.60%

key_files.role: Common fighter matching cleanup; rewrites item selection control flow, adds a terminal-velocity inline accessor, and expands an island collision check in ftCo_800AB224.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A61D8 uses continue guards, ftCo_800A5908, and itemDist
diff_excerpt: ftCo_800AB224 replaces ftCo_800A21FC call with explicit mpIsland_8005AB54/mpIsland_8005AC14 logic
text_corpus issue_comment: ftCo_800AB224 reaches 100%; ftCo_800A61D8 and ftCo_800A9904 improve

key_files.role: Small stack/declaration-order matching changes for Link/Samus hookshot-related functions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D8EC8 adds UNUSED u32 unused0
diff_excerpt: fn_800D9930 reorders frame/i declarations, changes PAD_STACK from 0x8 to 4, and moves Item* it declaration into branch
text_corpus issue_comment: fn_800D8EC8 reaches 100%; fn_800D9930 improves to 98.80%

key_files.role: Kirby SpecialN matching changes; adjusts table signedness, wraps a function body in a static inline helper, extracts joint-reference loop helper, and tunes PAD_STACK/declaration order.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_Init_803CB46C changes from s8 to u8
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50 body moved into ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50_inline wrapper
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs extracted from loop over joints and FighterBone parts
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C PAD_STACK changes
text_corpus issue_comment: Kirby functions ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50, ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C improve but are not listed as new 100% matches

what_changed: PR body states this slice exact-matches 4 newly matched functions across 4 files, with runner validation and clean regressions.
Bot report lists 4 new matches: ftColl_8007925C, ftColl_80077970, ftCo_800AB224, and fn_800D8EC8.
Bot report lists 8 improvements in unmatched items, including ftColl_80077C60, fn_800D9930, multiple Kirby SpecialN functions, ftCo_800A61D8, and ftCo_800A9904.
ftCo_800A61D8 changed from nested relevant/prio conditionals to guard-continue filtering using ftCo_800A5908 and itemDist.
ftCo_800AB224 replaced a call to ftCo_800A21FC with explicit ground/island collision logic and corresponding stack/local changes.
ftColl_80077970 was split into two static inline helpers, inlineItemA0 and inlineItemA1, while preserving midpoint/effect-spawn logic in the wrapper.
ftcoll.c .sdata2 constants were changed from early definitions to early extern declarations plus later definitions after ftColl_80077970.
ftkirby.c introduced static inline helper extraction for ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50 and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 joint-reference insertion, plus PAD_STACK and local-order changes.

smart_moves.text: Flattened nested item-selection logic in ftCo_800A61D8 into guard-continue checks, reducing temporary flag variables while preserving the candidate-selection flow.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A61D8 removes relevant/prio and adds continue guards

smart_moves.text: Used narrow static inline helpers to isolate codegen-sensitive subblocks in ftColl_80077970 and Kirby SpecialN without changing public call surfaces.
Kept verifier evidence explicit in the PR body and bot report: isolated build, clean regression report, and per-symbol match/improvement table.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For codegen-sensitive matching, extracting a repeated or bulky subblock into a static inline helper can change the wrapper shape enough to close or improve a match, but should be tied to verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftColl_80077970 introduces inlineItemA0/inlineItemA1 and bot reports 100%
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs and bot reports improvement

decomp_lessons.lesson: Guard-continue control flow can be a useful alternative to deeply nested generated-looking conditionals when filtering candidates in loops.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_800A61D8 removes nested relevant/prio ladder and uses continue guards
text_corpus issue_comment: ftCo_800A61D8 improves from 98.82% to 99.11%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small stack-shape and declaration-lifetime changes, including PAD_STACK size and moving locals into narrower scopes, were used in this PR for score improvements and one new exact match; these remain matching tactics requiring verification rather than broad style guidance.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_800D9930 PAD_STACK(4) and branch-local Item* it

naming_conventions.text: Observed inline helper names sometimes preserve the owning function name plus `_inline` or `_inline0`, e.g. ftKb_SpecialN_800EED50_inline and ftCo_800A9904_inline0.
Observed short local role names include fp, ip, cur, item, hit, ef_pos, part_off, sp28, and sp24; the PR does not provide enough evidence to promote these beyond local practice.
Address-style global/function names are retained for unclear semantics, such as ftColl_804D82E0 and ftCo_800AB224.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Static inline extraction to alter inlining and local code shape while preserving a public wrapper.
PAD_STACK tuning and local declaration reordering were used in several touched functions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C adds PAD_STACK(4) and moves idx declaration

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Data placement was adjusted in ftcoll.c by declaring .sdata2 constants as extern near the top and defining them later after ftColl_80077970.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftcoll.c .sdata2 declarations changed to extern
diff_excerpt: later .sdata2 definitions inserted after ftColl_80077970

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicit field and helper access was substituted for prior temporary variables or helper calls where it improved matching, e.g. ftCo_800A9904_inline0 and explicit mpIsland checks.

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded slice.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: only pr_body and decomp-dev[bot] issue_comment loaded

review_feedback.text: Automated bot feedback reports 4 new matches, 8 improvements, and matched code increase of +5812 bytes; this is validation/report evidence, not reviewer intent.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for inlineItemA0 inlineItemA1 to see whether ftColl_80077970 helper names survived review.
Search later PRs for ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs to see whether extracted joint traversal became accepted or was cleaned up.
Search ftcoll.c history for ftColl_804D82E0 .sdata2 extern definitions to determine whether this data-placement tactic was retained.
Search later comments on PR #2655 for human review after the loaded slice timestamp, because current slice has no review comments.
Search for ftCo_800AB224 and ftCo_800A21FC in later source to determine whether the explicit mpIsland expansion was retained or reabstracted.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Static inline extraction as a narrow matching tactic
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2655 shows static inline extraction used in ftColl_80077970 and Kirby SpecialN functions, with bot-reported exact matches or improvements. Treat as a tactic requiring verifier evidence, not as unconditional style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Guard-continue loop filters can replace nested generated conditionals
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftCo_800A61D8, nested item relevance/prio logic was rewritten into continue guards and helper calls, with the bot reporting a score improvement for the function.

source_update_candidates.title: ftcoll.c .sdata2 constants placement around ftColl_80077970
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2655, ftcoll.c changed ftColl_804D82E0 through ftColl_804D831C from early .sdata2 definitions to early extern declarations with definitions placed after ftColl_80077970. Curator should verify against current source/history before recording as a path fact.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftcoll.c top .sdata2 block changed to extern declarations
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible file-specific data-placement fact, but it may be a temporary matching tactic and the PR is still open in the loaded metadata.

source_update_candidates.title: Inline-helper extraction should remain evidence-gated
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2655 provides another example where static inline helpers and wrapper functions were used for matching. Existing standards already caution that manual inline expansion/tactics need evidence; curator may consider whether inline-helper extraction should be named explicitly if similar PRs accumulate.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential refinement to existing matching-tactics guidance; one PR alone is not enough to promote a new global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK size choices from this PR as general style; they are local codegen tactics with verifier evidence only for this slice.
Do not infer semantic names for address-style symbols such as ftColl_804D82E0 or ftCo_800AB224; the PR keeps address-style names.
Do not treat the bot report as human review approval or merge acceptance.
Do not conclude that data-section movement is broadly acceptable; ftcoll.c data relocation needs source/path verification and may be specific to this match.
